 `https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo`

```text
# Ruflo v3: Enterprise AI Orchestration Platform

## Purpose
Ruflo (formerly Claude Flow) is a production-ready agent orchestration platform enabling deployment of 60+ specialized AI agents working in coordinated swarms. It transforms Claude Code into a multi-agent development system for complex software engineering tasks.

## Core Architecture

**System Flow:**
User → CLI/MCP → Router → Swarm → Agents → Memory → LLM Providers, with a feedback learning loop

**Key Layers:**
- **Entry Layer:** CLI, MCP Server, AIDefence security
- **Routing Layer:** Q-Learning Router, MoE (8 experts), 42+ skills, 17 hooks
- **Swarm Coordination:** Hierarchical/mesh topologies, Raft/Byzantine/Gossip consensus
- **60+ Specialized Agents:** Coders, testers, reviewers, architects, security specialists
- **RuVector Intelligence:** SONA optimization, Flash Attention, HNSW search, ReasoningBank, LoRA fine-tuning

## Tech Stack

**Runtime:** Node.js 20+, TypeScript  
**Core Technologies:** WebAssembly (policy engine), ONNX Runtime, PostgreSQL with vectors  
**LLM Providers:** Claude, GPT, Gemini, Cohere, Ollama  
**Vector Search:** HNSW (150-12,500x faster), RuVector PostgreSQL  
**Learning:** 9 RL algorithms (Q-Learning, PPO, DQN), EWC++ (prevents forgetting)

## Main Directory Structure

```
/ruflo              - Core platform
/v2, /v3            - Version-specific implementations
/agents             - 60+ agent definitions
/plugin             - Plugin SDK framework
/packages/coflow    - Workflow orchestration
/docs/ruvector-postgres - Vector DB documentation
/tests              - Test suites
/scripts            - Installation and setup scripts
/bin                - CLI executables
```

## Key Features

**Agent Capabilities:**
- Self-learning architecture adapting via SONA (<0.05ms)
- Distributed swarm coordination with fault tolerance
- Collective memory with HNSW retrieval (~61µs search)
- Knowledge graph with PageRank influence detection

**Performance Optimizations:**
- Agent Booster (WASM): 352x faster for simple code transforms
- Token Optimizer: 30-50% cost reduction via compression/caching
- Smart routing: Skips expensive LLMs for straightforward tasks
- Parallel agent spawning with hierarchical coordination

**Security & Operations:**
- CVE-hardened with input validation, bcrypt, path traversal prevention
- 12 background workers for audits, optimization, learning
- Session persistence and export
- GitHub integration (PRs, issues, workflows)

## APIs & SDKs

**MCP Integration:** Native Claude Code plugin via Model Context Protocol  
**Plugin SDK:** Custom worker, hook, provider, security module development  
**Token Optimizer API:** `getCompactContext()`, `optimizedEdit()`, `getOptimalConfig()`  
**CLI:** `npx ruflo@latest` commands (init, hooks intelligence, agent management)

## Notable Files

- `README.md` - Comprehensive documentation
- `AGENTS.md` - Agent specifications
- `CLAUDE.md` / `CLAUDE.local.md` - Claude Code integration guides
- `package.json` - Dependencies and scripts
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration

## Licensing

MIT License - open source with commercial support available
```
</details></tool-use>

 `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ruvnet/ruflo/main/README.md`

```text
# Ruflo v3: Enterprise AI Orchestration Platform - Summary

## Overview
Ruflo v3 is a comprehensive multi-agent AI orchestration framework built on Anthropic's Claude. It enables teams to deploy, coordinate, and optimize specialized AI agents working together on complex software engineering tasks with self-learning capabilities.

## Key Installation Methods

### Quick Start (Recommended)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/ruvnet/claude-flow@main/scripts/install.sh | bash
```

### NPX Installation
```bash
npx ruflo@latest init --wizard
```

### Global Installation
```bash
npm install -g ruflo@latest
ruflo init
```

## Core Capabilities

**60+ Specialized Agents** across development categories including coding, testing, security, and DevOps. Agents organize into coordinated swarms with shared memory and collective decision-making.

**Self-Learning Architecture** featuring SONA (Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture) that adapts routing based on successful patterns, with EWC++ preventing catastrophic forgetting of learned knowledge.

**Multi-Provider LLM Support** integrating Claude, GPT, Gemini, Cohere, and local Llama models with automatic failover and intelligent cost-based routing.

## MCP Server Setup

### Claude Code Integration
```bash
claude mcp add ruflo -- npx ruflo@latest mcp start
```

### Configuration for Other Platforms
Ruflo supports MCP in Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and ChatGPT through standard MCP configuration files. Each platform uses environment variables for API keys and tool group filtering.

### Environment Variables
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` - Required for Claude models
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` - Optional for GPT models
- `CLAUDE_FLOW_TOOL_GROUPS` - Comma-separated tool groups to enable
- `CLAUDE_FLOW_TOOL_MODE` - Preset mode (develop, pr-review, devops)

## Plugin System

### Building Custom Plugins
The PluginBuilder API enables creating MCP tools, hooks, workers, and providers with fluent configuration. Performance targets include <20ms plugin load time and <0.5ms hook execution.

### Available Plugins
- **@claude-flow/plugin-agentic-qe** - Quality engineering with 58 agents
- **@claude-flow/plugin-prime-radiant** - Mathematical AI interpretability
- **@claude-flow/plugin-gastown-bridge** - WASM-accelerated orchestration
- Domain-specific: healthcare, financial, legal plugins available

Installation: `npm install @claude-flow/plugin-[name]`

## Essential CLI Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `init` | Project setup with wizard |
| `agent spawn` | Create specialized agents |
| `swarm init` | Initialize coordination topology |
| `memory search` | Vector-based pattern retrieval |
| `mcp start` | Launch MCP server |
| `hooks route` | Configure intelligent routing |
| `hive-mind spawn` | Queen-led multi-agent coordination |

## Memory & Intelligence

**HNSW Vector Search** provides sub-millisecond pattern retrieval with 150x-12,500x speedup versus traditional methods.

**AgentDB Integration** offers hierarchical memory with episodic/semantic consolidation, 20+ controllers, and automatic learning from task outcomes.

**RuVector PostgreSQL Bridge** enables production vector database operations with 39 attention mechanisms and 15 GNN layer types in SQL.

## Security Features

Input validation blocks prompt injection, path traversal, and command injection. AIDefence threat detection runs in <10ms. CVE monitoring and cryptographic proof chains ensure integrity.

## Performance Optimization

**Agent Booster (WASM)** handles simple code transforms in <1ms without LLM calls, supporting intents like var-to-const, add-types, and async-await conversion.

**Token Optimizer** reduces API costs 30-50% through context compression, intelligent caching, and right-sized model selection.

**3-Tier Model Routing** automatically routes simple tasks to WASM (free), medium complexity to Haiku/Sonnet ($0.0002-$0.003), and complex architecture work to Opus.

## Swarm Coordination

Four topology options support different workloads: hierarchical (clear authority), mesh (peer-to-peer), ring (sequential), and star (hub-spoke). Consensus mechanisms include Byzantine fault tolerance (handles 1/3 failing agents), Raft, and Gossip protocols.

The Hive Mind system uses strategic/tactical/adaptive queens coordinating 8 worker specializations with Byzantine-resistant collective memory.

## Background Automation

Twelve context-triggered workers automatically handle security audits, performance optimization, memory consolidation, documentation, refactoring detection, and benchmarking based on file changes and detected patterns.

## Configuration Best Practices

Store API keys in environment variables or `.env` files (add to `.gitignore`). Use tool group presets to reduce token overhead. Configure provider fallback chains for resilience. Enable compression for large codebases.

## Learning Capabilities

The system stores successful patterns in HNSW-indexed memory with automatic retrieval during similar tasks. Pattern storage includes trajectory data, confidence scores with Ebbinghaus forgetting curves, and cross-session persistence via SQLite and AgentDB.
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→# Claude Code Configuration - Ruflo v3.5
     2→
     3→> **Ruflo v3.5** (2026-02-27) — First major stable release. Formerly "Claude Flow".
     4→> 5,800+ commits, 55 alpha iterations, 215 MCP tools, 60+ agents, 8 AgentDB controllers.
     5→> Packages: `@claude-flow/cli@3.5.0`, `claude-flow@3.5.0`, `ruflo@3.5.0`
     6→
     7→## Behavioral Rules (Always Enforced)
     8→
     9→- Do what has been asked; nothing more, nothing less
    10→- NEVER create files unless they're absolutely necessary for achieving your goal
    11→- ALWAYS prefer editing an existing file to creating a new one
    12→- NEVER proactively create documentation files (*.md) or README files unless explicitly requested
    13→- NEVER save working files, text/mds, or tests to the root folder
    14→- Never continuously check status after spawning a swarm — wait for results
    15→- ALWAYS read a file before editing it
    16→- NEVER commit secrets, credentials, or .env files
    17→
    18→## File Organization
    19→
    20→- NEVER save to root folder — use the directories below
    21→- Use `/src` for source code files
    22→- Use `/tests` for test files
    23→- Use `/docs` for documentation and markdown files
    24→- Use `/config` for configuration files
    25→- Use `/scripts` for utility scripts
    26→- Use `/examples` for example code
    27→
    28→## Project Architecture
    29→
    30→- Follow Domain-Driven Design with bounded contexts
    31→- Keep files under 500 lines
    32→- Use typed interfaces for all public APIs
    33→- Prefer TDD London School (mock-first) for new code
    34→- Use event sourcing for state changes
    35→- Ensure input validation at system boundaries
    36→
    37→### Key Packages
    38→
    39→| Package | Path | Purpose |
    40→|---------|------|---------|
    41→| `@claude-flow/cli` | `v3/@claude-flow/cli/` | CLI entry point (26 commands) |
    42→| `@claude-flow/codex` | `v3/@claude-flow/codex/` | Dual-mode Claude + Codex collaboration |
    43→| `@claude-flow/guidance` | `v3/@claude-flow/guidance/` | Governance control plane |
    44→| `@claude-flow/hooks` | `v3/@claude-flow/hooks/` | 17 hooks + 12 workers |
    45→| `@claude-flow/memory` | `v3/@claude-flow/memory/` | AgentDB + HNSW search |
    46→| `@claude-flow/security` | `v3/@claude-flow/security/` | Input validation, CVE remediation |
    47→
    48→## Concurrency: 1 MESSAGE = ALL RELATED OPERATIONS
    49→
    50→- All operations MUST be concurrent/parallel in a single message
    51→- Use Claude Code's Task tool for spawning agents, not just MCP
    52→
    53→**Mandatory patterns:**
    54→- ALWAYS batch ALL todos in ONE TodoWrite call (5-10+ minimum)
    55→- ALWAYS spawn ALL agents in ONE message with full instructions via Task tool
    56→- ALWAYS batch ALL file reads/writes/edits in ONE message
    57→- ALWAYS batch ALL terminal operations in ONE Bash message
    58→- ALWAYS batch ALL memory store/retrieve operations in ONE message
    59→
    60→---
    61→
    62→## Swarm Orchestration
    63→
    64→- MUST initialize the swarm using MCP tools when starting complex tasks
    65→- MUST spawn concurrent agents using Claude Code's Task tool
    66→- Never use MCP tools alone for execution — Task tool agents do the actual work
    67→
    68→### MCP + Task Tool in SAME Message
    69→
    70→- MUST call MCP tools AND Task tool in ONE message for complex work
    71→- Always call MCP first, then IMMEDIATELY call Task tool to spawn agents
    72→
    73→### 3-Tier Model Routing (ADR-026)
    74→
    75→| Tier | Handler | Latency | Cost | Use Cases |
    76→|------|---------|---------|------|-----------|
    77→| **1** | Agent Booster (WASM) | <1ms | $0 | Simple transforms (var→const, add types, etc.) — **Skip LLM entirely** |
    78→| **2** | Haiku | ~500ms | $0.0002 | Simple tasks, low complexity (<30%) |
    79→| **3** | Sonnet/Opus | 2-5s | $0.003-0.015 | Complex reasoning, architecture, security (>30%) |
    80→
    81→- Always check for `[AGENT_BOOSTER_AVAILABLE]` or `[TASK_MODEL_RECOMMENDATION]` before spawning agents
    82→- Use Edit tool directly when `[AGENT_BOOSTER_AVAILABLE]` — intent types: `var-to-const`, `add-types`, `add-error-handling`, `async-await`, `add-logging`, `remove-console`
    83→
    84→## Swarm Configuration & Anti-Drift
    85→
    86→### Anti-Drift Coding Swarm (PREFERRED DEFAULT)
    87→
    88→- ALWAYS use hierarchical topology for coding swarms
    89→- Keep maxAgents at 6-8 for tight coordination
    90→- Use specialized strategy for clear role boundaries
    91→- Use `raft` consensus for hive-mind (leader maintains authoritative state)
    92→- Run frequent checkpoints via `post-task` hooks
    93→- Keep shared memory namespace for all agents
    94→- Keep task cycles short with verification gates
    95→
    96→```javascript
    97→mcp__ruv-swarm__swarm_init({
    98→  topology: "hierarchical",
    99→  maxAgents: 8,
   100→  strategy: "specialized"
   101→})
   102→```
   103→
   104→## Dual-Mode Collaboration (Claude Code + Codex)
   105→
   106→This repository uses **dual-mode orchestration** to run Claude Code (🔵) and OpenAI Codex (🟢) workers in parallel with shared memory coordination. Both platforms collaborate on development tasks with cross-learning.
   107→
   108→### Why Dual-Mode?
   109→
   110→| Single Platform | Dual-Mode Collaboration |
   111→|----------------|------------------------|
   112→| One model's perspective | Two AI platforms cross-validating |
   113→| Limited reasoning styles | Complementary strengths |
   114→| No external verification | Built-in code review |
   115→| Sequential workflows | Parallel execution |
   116→
   117→### Dual-Mode Swarm Protocol
   118→
   119→For complex tasks, spawn both Claude and Codex workers in parallel:
   120→
   121→```javascript
   122→// STEP 1: Initialize dual-mode swarm
   123→mcp__ruv-swarm__swarm_init({
   124→  topology: "hierarchical",
   125→  maxAgents: 8,
   126→  strategy: "specialized"
   127→})
   128→
   129→// STEP 2: Spawn BOTH platforms in parallel via Task tool
   130→// 🔵 Claude Code workers (architecture, security, testing)
   131→Task("Architect", "Design the implementation. Store design in memory namespace 'collaboration'.", "system-architect")
   132→Task("Tester", "Write tests based on architect's design. Read from 'collaboration' namespace.", "tester")
   133→Task("Reviewer", "Review code quality and security. Store findings in 'collaboration'.", "reviewer")
   134→
   135→// 🟢 Codex workers (implementation, optimization)
   136→// Spawn via CLI for Codex platform
   137→Bash("npx claude-flow-codex dual run --worker 'codex:coder:Implement the solution based on architect design' --namespace collaboration")
   138→Bash("npx claude-flow-codex dual run --worker 'codex:optimizer:Optimize performance based on implementation' --namespace collaboration")
   139→
   140→// STEP 3: Coordinate via shared memory
   141→Bash("npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory store --namespace collaboration --key 'task-context' --value '[task description]'")
   142→```
   143→
   144→### Collaboration Templates (Pre-Built Pipelines)
   145→
   146→| Template | Workers | Pipeline |
   147→|----------|---------|----------|
   148→| `feature` | 🔵 Architect → 🟢 Coder → 🔵 Tester → 🟢 Reviewer | Full feature development |
   149→| `security` | 🔵 Analyst → 🟢 Scanner → 🔵 Reporter | Security audit workflow |
   150→| `refactor` | 🔵 Architect → 🟢 Refactorer → 🔵 Tester | Code modernization |
   151→| `bugfix` | 🔵 Researcher → 🟢 Coder → 🔵 Tester | Bug investigation & fix |
   152→
   153→### Dual-Mode CLI Commands
   154→
   155→```bash
   156→# Run a collaboration template
   157→npx claude-flow-codex dual run feature --task "Add user authentication with OAuth"
   158→npx claude-flow-codex dual run security --target "./src"
   159→npx claude-flow-codex dual run refactor --target "./src/legacy"
   160→
   161→# Custom multi-platform swarm
   162→npx claude-flow-codex dual run \
   163→  --worker "claude:architect:Design the API structure" \
   164→  --worker "codex:coder:Implement REST endpoints" \
   165→  --worker "claude:tester:Write integration tests" \
   166→  --worker "codex:reviewer:Review code quality" \
   167→  --namespace "api-feature"
   168→
   169→# Check collaboration status
   170→npx claude-flow-codex dual status
   171→
   172→# List available templates
   173→npx claude-flow-codex dual templates
   174→```
   175→
   176→### Shared Memory Coordination
   177→
   178→All workers share state via the `collaboration` namespace:
   179→
   180→```bash
   181→# Store context for cross-platform sharing
   182→npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory store --namespace collaboration --key "design-decisions" --value "..."
   183→
   184→# Search for patterns across all workers
   185→npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory search --namespace collaboration --query "authentication patterns"
   186→
   187→# Retrieve specific findings
   188→npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory retrieve --namespace collaboration --key "security-findings"
   189→```
   190→
   191→### Cross-Platform Learning
   192→
   193→Both platforms learn from each other's outputs:
   194→
   195→```bash
   196→# After successful collaboration, train patterns
   197→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks post-task --task-id "dual-[id]" --success true --train-neural true
   198→
   199→# Store successful collaboration patterns
   200→npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory store --namespace patterns --key "dual-mode-[pattern]" --value "[what worked]"
   201→
   202→# Transfer learnings to both platforms
   203→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks transfer store --pattern "dual-collab-success"
   204→```
   205→
   206→### Worker Dependency Levels
   207→
   208→Workers execute in dependency order:
   209→
   210→```
   211→Level 0: [🔵 Architect]           # No dependencies - runs first
   212→Level 1: [🟢 Coder, 🔵 Tester]    # Depends on Architect
   213→Level 2: [🔵 Reviewer]            # Depends on Coder + Tester
   214→Level 3: [🟢 Optimizer]           # Depends on Reviewer approval
   215→```
   216→
   217→### Platform Strengths
   218→
   219→| Task Type | Preferred Platform | Reason |
   220→|-----------|-------------------|--------|
   221→| Architecture & Design | 🔵 Claude | Strong reasoning, system thinking |
   222→| Implementation | 🟢 Codex | Fast code generation |
   223→| Security Review | 🔵 Claude | Careful analysis, threat modeling |
   224→| Performance Optimization | 🟢 Codex | Code-level optimizations |
   225→| Testing Strategy | 🔵 Claude | Coverage analysis, edge cases |
   226→| Refactoring | 🟢 Codex | Bulk code transformations |
   227→
   228→### Programmatic API
   229→
   230→```typescript
   231→import { DualModeOrchestrator, CollaborationTemplates } from '@claude-flow/codex';
   232→
   233→const orchestrator = new DualModeOrchestrator({
   234→  namespace: 'my-feature',
   235→  memoryBackend: 'hybrid'
   236→});
   237→
   238→// Use pre-built template
   239→const workers = CollaborationTemplates.featureDevelopment('Add OAuth login');
   240→
   241→// Run collaboration
   242→const results = await orchestrator.runCollaboration(workers, 'Implement OAuth feature');
   243→
   244→// Access shared memory
   245→const designDocs = await orchestrator.getMemory('design-decisions');
   246→```
   247→
   248→---
   249→
   250→## Swarm Protocols & Routing
   251→
   252→### Auto-Start Swarm Protocol
   253→
   254→When the user requests a complex task (multi-file changes, feature implementation, refactoring), **immediately execute this pattern in a SINGLE message:**
   255→
   256→```javascript
   257→// STEP 1: Initialize swarm coordination via MCP (in parallel with agent spawning)
   258→mcp__ruv-swarm__swarm_init({
   259→  topology: "hierarchical",
   260→  maxAgents: 8,
   261→  strategy: "specialized"
   262→})
   263→
   264→// STEP 2: Spawn agents concurrently using Claude Code's Task tool
   265→// ALL Task calls MUST be in the SAME message for parallel execution
   266→Task("Coordinator", "You are the swarm coordinator. Initialize session, coordinate other agents via memory. Run: npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks session-start", "hierarchical-coordinator")
   267→Task("Researcher", "Analyze requirements and existing code patterns. Store findings in memory via hooks.", "researcher")
   268→Task("Architect", "Design implementation approach based on research. Document decisions in memory.", "system-architect")
   269→Task("Coder", "Implement the solution following architect's design. Coordinate via hooks.", "coder")
   270→Task("Tester", "Write tests for the implementation. Report coverage via hooks.", "tester")
   271→Task("Reviewer", "Review code quality and security. Document findings.", "reviewer")
   272→
   273→// STEP 3: Batch all todos
   274→TodoWrite({ todos: [
   275→  {content: "Initialize swarm coordination", status: "in_progress", activeForm: "Initializing swarm"},
   276→  {content: "Research and analyze requirements", status: "in_progress", activeForm: "Researching requirements"},
   277→  {content: "Design architecture", status: "pending", activeForm: "Designing architecture"},
   278→  {content: "Implement solution", status: "pending", activeForm: "Implementing solution"},
   279→  {content: "Write tests", status: "pending", activeForm: "Writing tests"},
   280→  {content: "Review and finalize", status: "pending", activeForm: "Reviewing code"}
   281→]})
   282→
   283→// STEP 4: Store swarm state in memory
   284→mcp__claude-flow__memory_usage({
   285→  action: "store",
   286→  namespace: "swarm",
   287→  key: "current-session",
   288→  value: JSON.stringify({task: "[user's task]", agents: 6, startedAt: new Date().toISOString()})
   289→})
   290→```
   291→
   292→### Agent Routing (Anti-Drift)
   293→
   294→| Code | Task | Agents |
   295→|------|------|--------|
   296→| 1 | Bug Fix | coordinator, researcher, coder, tester |
   297→| 3 | Feature | coordinator, architect, coder, tester, reviewer |
   298→| 5 | Refactor | coordinator, architect, coder, reviewer |
   299→| 7 | Performance | coordinator, perf-engineer, coder |
   300→| 9 | Security | coordinator, security-architect, auditor |
   301→| 11 | Memory | coordinator, memory-specialist, perf-engineer |
   302→| 13 | Docs | researcher, api-docs |
   303→
   304→**Codes 1-11: hierarchical/specialized (anti-drift). Code 13: mesh/balanced**
   305→
   306→### Task Complexity Detection
   307→
   308→**AUTO-INVOKE SWARM when task involves:**
   309→- Multiple files (3+)
   310→- New feature implementation
   311→- Refactoring across modules
   312→- API changes with tests
   313→- Security-related changes
   314→- Performance optimization
   315→- Database schema changes
   316→
   317→**SKIP SWARM for:**
   318→- Single file edits
   319→- Simple bug fixes (1-2 lines)
   320→- Documentation updates
   321→- Configuration changes
   322→- Quick questions/exploration
   323→
   324→## Project Configuration
   325→
   326→This project is configured with Claude Flow V3 (Anti-Drift Defaults):
   327→- **Topology**: hierarchical (prevents drift via central coordination)
   328→- **Max Agents**: 8 (smaller team = less drift)
   329→- **Strategy**: specialized (clear roles, no overlap)
   330→- **Consensus**: raft (leader maintains authoritative state)
   331→- **Memory Backend**: hybrid (SQLite + AgentDB)
   332→- **HNSW Indexing**: Enabled (150x-12,500x faster)
   333→- **Neural Learning**: Enabled (SONA)
   334→
   335→## V3 CLI Commands (26 Commands, 140+ Subcommands)
   336→
   337→### Core Commands
   338→
   339→| Command | Subcommands | Description |
   340→|---------|-------------|-------------|
   341→| `init` | 4 | Project initialization with wizard, presets, skills, hooks |
   342→| `agent` | 8 | Agent lifecycle (spawn, list, status, stop, metrics, pool, health, logs) |
   343→| `swarm` | 6 | Multi-agent swarm coordination and orchestration |
   344→| `memory` | 11 | AgentDB memory with vector search (150x-12,500x faster) |
   345→| `mcp` | 9 | MCP server management and tool execution |
   346→| `task` | 6 | Task creation, assignment, and lifecycle |
   347→| `session` | 7 | Session state management and persistence |
   348→| `config` | 7 | Configuration management and provider setup |
   349→| `status` | 3 | System status monitoring with watch mode |
   350→| `start` | 3 | Service startup and quick launch |
   351→| `workflow` | 6 | Workflow execution and template management |
   352→| `hooks` | 17 | Self-learning hooks + 12 background workers |
   353→| `hive-mind` | 6 | Queen-led Byzantine fault-tolerant consensus |
   354→
   355→### Advanced Commands
   356→
   357→| Command | Subcommands | Description |
   358→|---------|-------------|-------------|
   359→| `daemon` | 5 | Background worker daemon (start, stop, status, trigger, enable) |
   360→| `neural` | 5 | Neural pattern training (train, status, patterns, predict, optimize) |
   361→| `security` | 6 | Security scanning (scan, audit, cve, threats, validate, report) |
   362→| `performance` | 5 | Performance profiling (benchmark, profile, metrics, optimize, report) |
   363→| `providers` | 5 | AI providers (list, add, remove, test, configure) |
   364→| `plugins` | 5 | Plugin management (list, install, uninstall, enable, disable) |
   365→| `deployment` | 5 | Deployment management (deploy, rollback, status, environments, release) |
   366→| `embeddings` | 4 | Vector embeddings (embed, batch, search, init) - 75x faster with agentic-flow |
   367→| `claims` | 4 | Claims-based authorization (check, grant, revoke, list) |
   368→| `migrate` | 5 | V2 to V3 migration with rollback support |
   369→| `process` | 4 | Background process management |
   370→| `doctor` | 1 | System diagnostics with health checks |
   371→| `completions` | 4 | Shell completions (bash, zsh, fish, powershell) |
   372→
   373→### Quick CLI Examples
   374→
   375→```bash
   376→# Initialize project
   377→npx claude-flow@v3alpha init --wizard
   378→
   379→# Start daemon with background workers
   380→npx claude-flow@v3alpha daemon start
   381→
   382→# Spawn an agent
   383→npx claude-flow@v3alpha agent spawn -t coder --name my-coder
   384→
   385→# Initialize swarm
   386→npx claude-flow@v3alpha swarm init --v3-mode
   387→
   388→# Search memory (HNSW-indexed)
   389→npx claude-flow@v3alpha memory search -q "authentication patterns"
   390→
   391→# System diagnostics
   392→npx claude-flow@v3alpha doctor --fix
   393→
   394→# Security scan
   395→npx claude-flow@v3alpha security scan --depth full
   396→
   397→# Performance benchmark
   398→npx claude-flow@v3alpha performance benchmark --suite all
   399→```
   400→
   401→## Headless Background Instances (claude -p)
   402→
   403→Use `claude -p` (print/pipe mode) to spawn headless Claude instances for parallel background work. These run non-interactively and return results to stdout.
   404→
   405→### Basic Usage
   406→
   407→```bash
   408→# Single headless task
   409→claude -p "Analyze the authentication module for security issues"
   410→
   411→# With model selection
   412→claude -p --model haiku "Format this config file"
   413→claude -p --model opus "Design the database schema for user management"
   414→
   415→# With output format
   416→claude -p --output-format json "List all TODO comments in src/"
   417→claude -p --output-format stream-json "Refactor the error handling in api.ts"
   418→
   419→# With budget limits
   420→claude -p --max-budget-usd 0.50 "Run comprehensive security audit"
   421→
   422→# With specific tools allowed
   423→claude -p --allowedTools "Read,Grep,Glob" "Find all files that import the auth module"
   424→
   425→# Skip permissions (sandboxed environments only)
   426→claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions "Fix all lint errors in src/"
   427→```
   428→
   429→### Parallel Background Execution
   430→
   431→```bash
   432→# Spawn multiple headless instances in parallel
   433→claude -p "Analyze src/auth/ for vulnerabilities" &
   434→claude -p "Write tests for src/api/endpoints.ts" &
   435→claude -p "Review src/models/ for performance issues" &
   436→wait  # Wait for all to complete
   437→
   438→# With results captured
   439→SECURITY=$(claude -p "Security audit of auth module" &)
   440→TESTS=$(claude -p "Generate test coverage report" &)
   441→PERF=$(claude -p "Profile memory usage in workers" &)
   442→wait
   443→echo "$SECURITY" "$TESTS" "$PERF"
   444→```
   445→
   446→### Session Continuation
   447→
   448→```bash
   449→# Start a task, resume later
   450→claude -p --session-id "abc-123" "Start analyzing the codebase"
   451→claude -p --resume "abc-123" "Continue with the test files"
   452→
   453→# Fork a session for parallel exploration
   454→claude -p --resume "abc-123" --fork-session "Try approach A: event sourcing"
   455→claude -p --resume "abc-123" --fork-session "Try approach B: CQRS pattern"
   456→```
   457→
   458→### Key Flags
   459→
   460→| Flag | Purpose |
   461→|------|---------|
   462→| `-p, --print` | Non-interactive mode, print and exit |
   463→| `--model <model>` | Select model (haiku, sonnet, opus) |
   464→| `--output-format <fmt>` | Output: text, json, stream-json |
   465→| `--max-budget-usd <amt>` | Spending cap per invocation |
   466→| `--allowedTools <tools>` | Restrict available tools |
   467→| `--append-system-prompt` | Add custom instructions |
   468→| `--resume <id>` | Continue a previous session |
   469→| `--fork-session` | Branch from resumed session |
   470→| `--fallback-model <model>` | Auto-fallback if primary overloaded |
   471→| `--permission-mode <mode>` | acceptEdits, bypassPermissions, plan, etc. |
   472→| `--mcp-config <json>` | Load MCP servers from JSON |
   473→
   474→## Available Agents (60+ Types)
   475→
   476→### Core Development
   477→`coder`, `reviewer`, `tester`, `planner`, `researcher`
   478→
   479→### V3 Specialized Agents
   480→`security-architect`, `security-auditor`, `memory-specialist`, `performance-engineer`
   481→
   482→### @claude-flow/security Module
   483→CVE remediation, input validation, path security:
   484→- `InputValidator` — Zod-based validation at boundaries
   485→- `PathValidator` — Path traversal prevention
   486→- `SafeExecutor` — Command injection protection
   487→- `PasswordHasher` — bcrypt hashing
   488→- `TokenGenerator` — Secure token generation
   489→
   490→### Token Optimizer (Agent Booster)
   491→Integrates agentic-flow optimizations for 30-50% token reduction:
   492→```typescript
   493→import { getTokenOptimizer } from '@claude-flow/integration';
   494→const optimizer = await getTokenOptimizer();
   495→
   496→// Compact context (32% fewer tokens)
   497→const ctx = await optimizer.getCompactContext("auth patterns");
   498→
   499→// 352x faster edits = fewer retries
   500→await optimizer.optimizedEdit(file, old, new, "typescript");
   501→
   502→// Optimal config (100% success rate)
   503→const config = optimizer.getOptimalConfig(agentCount);
   504→```
   505→| Feature | Token Savings |
   506→|---------|---------------|
   507→| ReasoningBank retrieval | -32% |
   508→| Agent Booster edits | -15% |
   509→| Cache (95% hit rate) | -10% |
   510→| Optimal batch size | -20% |
   511→
   512→### Swarm Coordination
   513→`hierarchical-coordinator`, `mesh-coordinator`, `adaptive-coordinator`, `collective-intelligence-coordinator`, `swarm-memory-manager`
   514→
   515→### Consensus & Distributed
   516→`byzantine-coordinator`, `raft-manager`, `gossip-coordinator`, `consensus-builder`, `crdt-synchronizer`, `quorum-manager`, `security-manager`
   517→
   518→### Performance & Optimization
   519→`perf-analyzer`, `performance-benchmarker`, `task-orchestrator`, `memory-coordinator`, `smart-agent`
   520→
   521→### GitHub & Repository
   522→`github-modes`, `pr-manager`, `code-review-swarm`, `issue-tracker`, `release-manager`, `workflow-automation`, `project-board-sync`, `repo-architect`, `multi-repo-swarm`
   523→
   524→### SPARC Methodology
   525→`sparc-coord`, `sparc-coder`, `specification`, `pseudocode`, `architecture`, `refinement`
   526→
   527→### Specialized Development
   528→`backend-dev`, `mobile-dev`, `ml-developer`, `cicd-engineer`, `api-docs`, `system-architect`, `code-analyzer`, `base-template-generator`
   529→
   530→### Testing & Validation
   531→`tdd-london-swarm`, `production-validator`
   532→
   533→## Agent Teams (Multi-Agent Coordination)
   534→
   535→Claude Code's experimental Agent Teams feature is fully integrated with Claude Flow for advanced multi-agent coordination.
   536→
   537→### Enabling Agent Teams
   538→
   539→Agent Teams is automatically enabled when you run `npx claude-flow@v3alpha init`. The following is added to `.claude/settings.json`:
   540→
   541→```json
   542→{
   543→  "env": {
   544→    "CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS": "1"
   545→  },
   546→  "claudeFlow": {
   547→    "agentTeams": {
   548→      "enabled": true,
   549→      "teammateMode": "auto",
   550→      "taskListEnabled": true,
   551→      "mailboxEnabled": true
   552→    }
   553→  }
   554→}
   555→```
   556→
   557→### Agent Teams Components
   558→
   559→| Component | Tool | Purpose |
   560→|-----------|------|---------|
   561→| **Team Lead** | You (main Claude) | Coordinates teammates, assigns tasks, reviews results |
   562→| **Teammates** | `Task` tool | Sub-agents spawned to work on specific tasks |
   563→| **Task List** | `TaskCreate/TaskList/TaskUpdate` | Shared todo list visible to all team members |
   564→| **Mailbox** | `SendMessage` | Inter-agent messaging for coordination |
   565→
   566→### Creating and Managing Teams
   567→
   568→```javascript
   569→// Create a team
   570→TeamCreate({
   571→  team_name: "feature-dev",
   572→  description: "Building new feature",
   573→  agent_type: "coordinator"
   574→})
   575→
   576→// Create shared tasks
   577→TaskCreate({ subject: "Design API", description: "...", activeForm: "Designing" })
   578→TaskCreate({ subject: "Implement endpoints", description: "...", activeForm: "Implementing" })
   579→TaskCreate({ subject: "Write tests", description: "...", activeForm: "Testing" })
   580→
   581→// Spawn teammates (run in background for parallel work)
   582→Task({
   583→  prompt: "Design the API according to task #1...",
   584→  subagent_type: "system-architect",
   585→  team_name: "feature-dev",
   586→  name: "architect",
   587→  run_in_background: true
   588→})
   589→Task({
   590→  prompt: "Implement endpoints from task #2...",
   591→  subagent_type: "coder",
   592→  team_name: "feature-dev",
   593→  name: "developer",
   594→  run_in_background: true
   595→})
   596→```
   597→
   598→### Agent Teams Hooks
   599→
   600→| Hook | Trigger | Purpose |
   601→|------|---------|---------|
   602→| `TeammateIdle` | Teammate finishes turn | Auto-assign pending tasks to idle teammates |
   603→| `TaskCompleted` | Task marked complete | Train patterns from successful work, notify lead |
   604→
   605→### Hook Commands
   606→
   607→```bash
   608→# Handle idle teammate (auto-assigns available tasks)
   609→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks teammate-idle --auto-assign true
   610→
   611→# Handle task completion (trains patterns, notifies lead)
   612→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks task-completed -i task-123 --train-patterns true
   613→
   614→# Check on team progress
   615→TaskList
   616→
   617→# Send message to teammate
   618→SendMessage({
   619→  type: "message",
   620→  recipient: "developer",
   621→  content: "Please prioritize the auth endpoint",
   622→  summary: "Prioritize auth"
   623→})
   624→
   625→# Shutdown teammate gracefully
   626→SendMessage({
   627→  type: "shutdown_request",
   628→  recipient: "developer",
   629→  content: "Work complete, shutting down"
   630→})
   631→```
   632→
   633→### Best Practices for Agent Teams
   634→
   635→1. **Spawn teammates in background**: Use `run_in_background: true` for parallel work
   636→2. **Create tasks first**: Use TaskCreate before spawning teammates so they have work
   637→3. **Use descriptive names**: Name teammates by role (architect, developer, tester)
   638→4. **Don't poll status**: Wait for teammates to message back or complete
   639→5. **Graceful shutdown**: Always send shutdown_request before TeamDelete
   640→6. **Clean up**: Use TeamDelete after all teammates have shut down
   641→
   642→### Teammate Display Modes
   643→
   644→| Mode | Description |
   645→|------|-------------|
   646→| `auto` | Automatically selects best mode for environment |
   647→| `in-process` | Teammates run in same process (default for CI/background) |
   648→| `tmux` | Split-pane display in terminal (requires tmux) |
   649→
   650→## V3 Hooks System (17 Hooks + 12 Workers)
   651→
   652→### Hook Categories
   653→
   654→| Category | Hooks | Purpose |
   655→|----------|-------|---------|
   656→| **Core** | `pre-edit`, `post-edit`, `pre-command`, `post-command`, `pre-task`, `post-task` | Tool lifecycle |
   657→| **Session** | `session-start`, `session-end`, `session-restore`, `notify` | Context management |
   658→| **Intelligence** | `route`, `explain`, `pretrain`, `build-agents`, `transfer` | Neural learning |
   659→| **Learning** | `intelligence` (trajectory-start/step/end, pattern-store/search, stats, attention) | Reinforcement |
   660→| **Agent Teams** | `teammate-idle`, `task-completed` | Multi-agent coordination |
   661→
   662→### 12 Background Workers
   663→
   664→| Worker | Priority | Description |
   665→|--------|----------|-------------|
   666→| `ultralearn` | normal | Deep knowledge acquisition |
   667→| `optimize` | high | Performance optimization |
   668→| `consolidate` | low | Memory consolidation |
   669→| `predict` | normal | Predictive preloading |
   670→| `audit` | critical | Security analysis |
   671→| `map` | normal | Codebase mapping |
   672→| `preload` | low | Resource preloading |
   673→| `deepdive` | normal | Deep code analysis |
   674→| `document` | normal | Auto-documentation |
   675→| `refactor` | normal | Refactoring suggestions |
   676→| `benchmark` | normal | Performance benchmarking |
   677→| `testgaps` | normal | Test coverage analysis |
   678→
   679→### Essential Hook Commands
   680→
   681→```bash
   682→# Core hooks
   683→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks pre-task --description "[task]"
   684→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks post-task --task-id "[id]" --success true
   685→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks post-edit --file "[file]" --train-patterns
   686→
   687→# Session management
   688→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks session-start --session-id "[id]"
   689→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks session-end --export-metrics true
   690→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks session-restore --session-id "[id]"
   691→
   692→# Intelligence routing
   693→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks route --task "[task]"
   694→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks explain --topic "[topic]"
   695→
   696→# Neural learning
   697→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks pretrain --model-type moe --epochs 10
   698→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks build-agents --agent-types coder,tester
   699→
   700→# Background workers
   701→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks worker list
   702→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks worker dispatch --trigger audit
   703→npx claude-flow@v3alpha hooks worker status
   704→```
   705→
   706→## Intelligence System (RuVector)
   707→
   708→V3 includes the RuVector Intelligence System:
   709→- **SONA**: Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture (<0.05ms adaptation)
   710→- **MoE**: Mixture of Experts for specialized routing
   711→- **HNSW**: 150x-12,500x faster pattern search
   712→- **EWC++**: Elastic Weight Consolidation (prevents forgetting)
   713→- **Flash Attention**: 2.49x-7.47x speedup
   714→
   715→The 4-step intelligence pipeline:
   716→1. **RETRIEVE** — Fetch relevant patterns via HNSW
   717→2. **JUDGE** — Evaluate with verdicts (success/failure)
   718→3. **DISTILL** — Extract key learnings via LoRA
   719→4. **CONSOLIDATE** — Prevent catastrophic forgetting via EWC++
   720→
   721→## Embeddings Package (v3.0.0-alpha.12)
   722→
   723→Features:
   724→- **sql.js**: Cross-platform SQLite persistent cache (WASM, no native compilation)
   725→- **Document chunking**: Configurable overlap and size
   726→- **Normalization**: L2, L1, min-max, z-score
   727→- **Hyperbolic embeddings**: Poincare ball model for hierarchical data
   728→- **75x faster**: With agentic-flow ONNX integration
   729→- **Neural substrate**: Integration with RuVector
   730→
   731→## Hive-Mind Consensus
   732→
   733→### Topologies
   734→- `hierarchical` — Queen controls workers directly
   735→- `mesh` — Fully connected peer network
   736→- `hierarchical-mesh` — Hybrid (recommended)
   737→- `adaptive` — Dynamic based on load
   738→
   739→### Consensus Strategies
   740→- `byzantine` — BFT (tolerates f < n/3 faulty)
   741→- `raft` — Leader-based (tolerates f < n/2)
   742→- `gossip` — Epidemic for eventual consistency
   743→- `crdt` — Conflict-free replicated data types
   744→- `quorum` — Configurable quorum-based
   745→
   746→## V3 Performance Targets
   747→
   748→| Metric | Target | Status |
   749→|--------|--------|--------|
   750→| HNSW Search | 150x-12,500x faster | **Implemented** (persistent) |
   751→| Memory Reduction | 50-75% with quantization | **Implemented** (3.92x Int8) |
   752→| SONA Integration | Pattern learning | **Implemented** (ReasoningBank) |
   753→| Flash Attention | 2.49x-7.47x speedup | In progress |
   754→| MCP Response | <100ms | Achieved |
   755→| CLI Startup | <500ms | Achieved |
   756→| SONA Adaptation | <0.05ms | In progress |
   757→
   758→## Environment Variables
   759→
   760→```bash
   761→# Configuration
   762→CLAUDE_FLOW_CONFIG=./claude-flow.config.json
   763→CLAUDE_FLOW_LOG_LEVEL=info
   764→
   765→# Provider API Keys
   766→ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
   767→OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
   768→GOOGLE_API_KEY=...
   769→
   770→# MCP Server
   771→CLAUDE_FLOW_MCP_PORT=3000
   772→CLAUDE_FLOW_MCP_HOST=localhost
   773→CLAUDE_FLOW_MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio
   774→
   775→# Memory
   776→CLAUDE_FLOW_MEMORY_BACKEND=hybrid
   777→CLAUDE_FLOW_MEMORY_PATH=./data/memory
   778→```
   779→
   780→## Doctor Health Checks
   781→
   782→Run `npx claude-flow@v3alpha doctor` to check:
   783→- Node.js version (20+)
   784→- npm version (9+)
   785→- Git installation
   786→- Config file validity
   787→- Daemon status
   788→- Memory database
   789→- API keys
   790→- MCP servers
   791→- Disk space
   792→- TypeScript installation
   793→
   794→## Quick Setup
   795→
   796→```bash
   797→# Add MCP servers
   798→claude mcp add claude-flow npx claude-flow@v3alpha mcp start
   799→claude mcp add ruv-swarm npx ruv-swarm mcp start  # Optional
   800→claude mcp add flow-nexus npx flow-nexus@latest mcp start  # Optional
   801→
   802→# Start daemon
   803→npx claude-flow@v3alpha daemon start
   804→
   805→# Run doctor
   806→npx claude-flow@v3alpha doctor --fix
   807→```
   808→
   809→## Claude Code vs MCP Tools
   810→
   811→### Claude Code Handles ALL EXECUTION:
   812→- **Task tool**: Spawn and run agents concurrently
   813→- File operations (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep)
   814→- Code generation and programming
   815→- Bash commands and system operations
   816→- TodoWrite and task management
   817→- Git operations
   818→
   819→### MCP Tools ONLY COORDINATE:
   820→- Swarm initialization (topology setup)
   821→- Agent type definitions
   822→- Task orchestration
   823→- Memory management
   824→- Neural features
   825→- Performance tracking
   826→
   827→- Keep MCP for coordination strategy only — use Claude Code's Task tool for real execution
   828→
   829→## Publishing to npm
   830→
   831→### Publishing Rules
   832→
   833→- MUST publish ALL THREE packages when publishing CLI changes: `@claude-flow/cli`, `claude-flow`, AND `ruflo`
   834→- MUST update ALL dist-tags for ALL THREE packages after publishing
   835→- Publish order: `@claude-flow/cli` first, then `claude-flow` (umbrella), then `ruflo` (alias umbrella)
   836→- MUST run verification for ALL THREE before telling user publishing is complete
   837→
   838→```bash
   839→# STEP 1: Build and publish CLI
   840→cd v3/@claude-flow/cli
   841→npm version 3.0.0-alpha.XXX --no-git-tag-version
   842→npm run build
   843→npm publish --tag alpha
   844→npm dist-tag add @claude-flow/cli@3.0.0-alpha.XXX latest
   845→
   846→# STEP 2: Publish claude-flow umbrella
   847→cd /workspaces/claude-flow
   848→npm version 3.0.0-alpha.XXX --no-git-tag-version
   849→npm publish --tag v3alpha
   850→
   851→# STEP 3: Update ALL claude-flow umbrella tags (CRITICAL - DON'T SKIP!)
   852→npm dist-tag add claude-flow@3.0.0-alpha.XXX latest
   853→npm dist-tag add claude-flow@3.0.0-alpha.XXX alpha
   854→
   855→# STEP 4: Publish ruflo umbrella (CRITICAL - DON'T FORGET!)
   856→cd /workspaces/claude-flow/ruflo
   857→npm version 3.0.0-alpha.XXX --no-git-tag-version
   858→npm publish --tag alpha
   859→npm dist-tag add ruflo@3.0.0-alpha.XXX lat
